mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive direction in >65% of months where FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: An elevated unemployment rate signals weakening aggregate demand and deteriorating corporate profit margins, which systematically reduces S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in >70% of observations when UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or monthly Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all test windows.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly retail sales exceed 750 billion dollars, consumer spending momentum accelerates corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >60% of triggered months, or the threshold effect vanishes when RSAFS growth rate falls below 2% year-over-year.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production above 105 indicates robust manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side expansion, which sustains positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns turn negative in >55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when capacity utilization drops below 78%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in the federal funds rate and decreases in the unemployment rate indicate a strong labor market absorbing monetary tightening, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative returns in >65% of cases, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in multivariate regression over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent high inflation and elevated policy rates compress real discount rates and squeeze profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered periods, or the interaction effect reverses direction when real GDP growth exceeds 3.0% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150 thousand persons signals robust economic expansion, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative returns in >50% of triggered months, or predictive accuracy drops to random chance (Rank IC < 0.01) when the yield curve steepens by >100bps.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts reflects weakening residential investment and broader credit contraction, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days during post-shock recovery phases.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >65% of triggered instances, or the housing signal loses explanatory power when commercial real estate lending expands concurrently.
